messages.content: Build a parent/child actor coordination machine in XState v5 using setup() with typed events and context. The parent invokes a worker child that processes a TASK and sends DONE back; the parent tracks how many tasks have been completed.
What's the v5 way to have a parent machine spawn a child on demand, send it a PING, and receive a PONG back? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how parent/child coordination works in XState v5, then show a working typed implementation where the child reports progress to the parent.

Write an XState v5 machine that coordinates a parent and a single invoked child where the parent requests COMPUTE and the child replies RESULT. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, actions declared in setup.
Add a cancellation capability to this v5 parent/child coordinator: when the parent receives CANCEL it should tell the child to STOP, and the child should acknowledge with STOPPED.

Build a cart-update machine in XState v5 using setup() with typed events and context. Use enqueueActions to bump the total, raise a CHECK event, and conditionally assign a coupon when the subtotal exceeds $100.
What's the v5 way to run several conditional side effects in one action (assign + raise + sendTo) using enqueueActions? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how enqueueActions works in XState v5, then show a working typed implementation that uses it to collapse four side effects into one action.
Write an XState v5 machine that debits an account and records an audit trail in a single action. Constraints: use enqueueActions with check(), strict types, no inline function guards outside setup.
Add an enqueueActions block to this v5 toggle machine so TOGGLE also bumps a counter, raises a LOG event, and (only when the machine was previously `off`) assigns a turnedOnAt timestamp.
Build a notification machine in XState v5 using setup() with typed events and context. It should emit a typed `notified` event whenever SHOW fires so subscribers can react via actor.on.
What's the v5 way to emit a typed side-channel event so external subscribers can listen with actor.on? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how emit() works in XState v5, then show a working typed implementation where a download machine emits `progress` and `completed` events.
Add an emit() capability to this v5 counter so every INCREMENT broadcasts a `changed` event with the new total for observers.
How do I declare a typed emitted event union in setup.types and emit from several transitions in v5? Brief example.

Build a media-player machine in XState v5 using setup() with typed events and context. It has playing/paused substates inside `active`, an `interrupted` sibling state, and a RESUME event that returns to the last substate via a shallow history node.
What's the v5 way to add a deep history state so returning to a compound parent restores the exact nested child that was active? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how shallow vs deep history states work in XState v5, then show a working typed implementation that uses shallow history.
Write an XState v5 machine for a chat widget that remembers the last open tab after being minimised. Constraints: shallow history, strict types, no inline guards.
Add a deep-history node to this v5 settings-modal machine so closing and reopening returns the user to the exact sub-page they were viewing.
How do I configure a v5 history state with a default target so first-time entry still works when there is no history yet? Show a minimal example.
Build a timer machine in XState v5 using setup() with typed events and context. It has a single `running` state; RESET should re-enter (fire entry actions again) while TICK should stay internal.
What's the v5 way to distinguish an internal self-transition from a re-entering one, and when should I use each? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how reenter: true differs from an internal transition in XState v5, then show a working typed implementation that uses both on the same state.
Write an XState v5 machine that treats REFRESH as a re-entering self-transition (so `entry` actions re-run) while keeping TOUCH internal. Constraints: strict types, single state node.
Build a user-fetch machine in XState v5 using setup() with typed events and context. It invokes a fromPromise actor whose input is a typed `{ userId: number }` computed from context.
What's the v5 way to pass typed input to an invoked fromPromise actor and read the typed output in onDone? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how input-typed actors work in XState v5, then show a working typed implementation that reads both context and event to build the actor input.
Write an XState v5 machine that invokes a search actor with typed input derived from context. Constraints: fromPromise<Output, Input>, input function reads only context, strict types.
Add an input-typed retry capability to this v5 machine: the invoked actor takes `{ userId: number; attempt: number }` and each retry bumps `attempt` in context before reinvocation.
How do I declare a fromPromise actor whose input depends on an event payload in v5? Brief example.

Build a checkout-summary machine in XState v5 using setup() with typed events and context. When the machine reaches `done`, it should return a typed `output` of `{ total: number; items: number }` readable via actor.getSnapshot().output.
What's the v5 way to consume a typed output from an invoked child machine in the parent's onDone transition? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how output typing works on a v5 root machine, then show a working typed implementation that exposes a final output readable from outside.

Write an XState v5 machine that invokes a child machine with a typed output and assigns only one field of that output into the parent context. Constraints: both machines strict-typed, child uses output function on a final state, parent consumes via onDone.
Add typed output to this v5 pipeline machine so that on reaching `done` the root actor exposes `{ processed: number; elapsedMs: number }` via getSnapshot().output.
